documentation.file_docstring: Marks every session entry in the store whose key contains {@link threadId} as "reset" by setting `updatedAt` to 0. This mirrors how the daily / idle session reset works: zeroing `updatedAt` makes `evaluateSessionFreshness` treat the session as stale on the next inbound message, so the bot starts a fresh conversation without deleting any on-disk transcript history.
inline_comments: Match when the threadId appears as a complete colon-separated segment.
e.g. "999" must be followed by ":" (middle) or end-of-string (final).
Using a regex avoids false-positives where one snowflake is a prefix of
another (e.g. searching for "999" must not match ":99900").
Resolve the store file. We pass `accountId` as `agentId` here to mirror
how other Discord subsystems resolve their per-account sessions stores.
Setting updatedAt to 0 signals that this session is stale.
evaluateSessionFreshness will create a new session on the next message.

functions.name: closeDiscordThreadSessions
